subject: Infrastructure adoption patterns - Celestia
body:
Hi Ismail,

Infrastructure companies like Celestia face the 'chicken and egg' problem - developers won't build until there's usage, users won't come until there are apps.

I was Director of Product at BlockSpaces (Web3 infra), now fractional Head of Product at Sharely Global. Have automated processes for Fortune 500s at Invoke Inc, now using OpenClaw for AI-driven product decisions.

Quick chat about breaking the adoption cycle?

Oskar
cal.com/oskarhurme
hook_used: Infrastructure companies like Celestia face the 'chicken and egg' problem - developers won't build until there's usage, users won't come until there are apps

subject: Developer onboarding insights - Moralis
body:
Hi Filip,

Most Web3 platforms lose 90% of developers in the first week - Moralis's SDK approach could be different.

I was Director of Product at BlockSpaces (Web3 infra), now fractional Head of Product at Sharely Global. Built automation systems for Fortune 500s at Invoke Inc, now using OpenClaw and latest AI tools for product optimization.

Quick call about developer retention strategy?

Oskar
cal.com/oskarhurme
hook_used: Most Web3 platforms lose 90% of developers in the first week - Moralis's SDK approach could be different

subject: DeFi retention beyond speculation - Zerion
body:
Hi Evgeny,

Zerion's portfolio approach is smart - most DeFi users are still just yield farming, not actually using the products.

I was Director of Product at BlockSpaces, now fractional Head of Product at Sharely Global (fintech in impact space). Built AI/automation systems for Fortune 500s, now applying similar approaches to Web3 product strategy.

Worth a 15-min call about user retention?

Oskar
cal.com/oskarhurme
hook_used: Zerion's portfolio approach is smart - most DeFi users are still just yield farming, not actually using the products

subject: Identity UX that users actually want - SpruceID
body:
Hi Wayne,

SpruceID's privacy-first approach is right - but most identity solutions feel like punishment to users instead of empowerment.

Fractional Head of Product at Sharely Global (fintech), previously Director of Product at BlockSpaces. Built user-friendly automation for Fortune 500s at Invoke Inc, now using OpenClaw for UX optimization.

Worth a call about making identity feel like a feature?

Oskar
cal.com/oskarhurme
hook_used: SpruceID's privacy-first approach is right - but most identity solutions feel like punishment to users instead of empowerment

subject: Analytics to product decisions - Dune Analytics
body:
Hi Mats,

Dune Analytics's data is impressive - the gap is usually between 'interesting insights' and 'what should we actually build differently'.

Fractional Head of Product at Sharely Global (fintech), previously built 99% accuracy ML models at Invoke Inc for Fortune 500s. Now using OpenClaw to automate data-to-product workflows.

Quick call about turning analytics into product wins?

Oskar
cal.com/oskarhurme
hook_used: Dune Analytics's data is impressive - the gap is usually between 'interesting insights' and 'what should we actually build differently'
